name: Stratford-sub-Castle
coordsSource: web-verified:GENUKI SU130326;Wikipedia grid SU133323 (~300m); listing-centroid n=35 ~1.3km SE toward Salisbury; 2026-06-04
overview: Stratford-sub-Castle is a small, pretty village and ancient parish that now forms a northern edge of Salisbury, about 2 miles from the city centre, set in the Avon valley beneath the Iron Age hillfort and ruined Norman castle of Old Sarum (from which it takes its name). The oldest building is the Grade I listed 13th-century Church of St Lawrence, near where the elder William Pitt (Earl of Chatham) was first elected to Parliament for the rotten borough of Old Sarum. It blends old thatched cottages with Georgian and modern homes and enjoys village character with city amenities close by.
character: A historic, sought-after village on Salisbury's northern fringe, combining riverside and downland scenery with the convenience of the city a short drive away.
amenities: St Lawrence Church (Grade I listed, 13th century)
Regular Salisbury Reds bus into the city
Avon Valley Nature Reserve (Wiltshire Wildlife Trust) nearby

schools.name: South Wilts Grammar School for Girls (and other Salisbury secondaries/grammars)

prices.summary: A desirable, historic village within Salisbury's boundary; thatched cottages, Georgian houses and family homes near Old Sarum command strong values given the location and grammar-school proximity. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: St Lawrence Church and its Pitt/Nelson connections
Walks in the Avon Valley Nature Reserve (otter sightings)
Easy access to Salisbury's cathedral, shops and culture
placesToEat: Wide choice in Salisbury city centre
pros: Village character with full city amenities under 2 miles away
Grade I church and Old Sarum heritage on the doorstep
Walking distance to South Wilts Grammar School
River Avon and nature reserve nearby
cons: Effectively a Salisbury suburb rather than a remote village
Limited dedicated amenities within the village
Close to the A36 ring road in places
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting village character with Salisbury's schools, station and shops within easy reach.
primarySupermarket: Convenience store on Castle Road; full supermarkets (John Lewis/Waitrose) in Salisbury
